scope_note: Implementation task breakdown only. No product code is modified by this artifact. Optional sandbox and AI SDK useChat work are deferred unless separately approved or required by a concrete product workflow.

strict_tdd.rule: For every runtime/API/frontend change, create or update failing tests before implementation. Implementation without relevant test delta is disallowed.

review_workload_forecast.estimated_changed_lines: 1200-1900 for non-deferred migration slices; +500-900 if sandbox is later approved
review_workload_forecast.suggested_split: Optional if the owner wants lower review load: PR 1 provider preflight + legacy adapter extraction -> PR 2 AI SDK text runtime + alert protocol cleanup -> PR 3 read-only tool loop -> PR 4 critical intent migration; sandbox/useChat remain separate approvals
notes: The user preflight chose single PR default and no fixed review budget, so implementation may proceed as one PR with slice commits and rollback checkpoints.
If the implementation approaches unreviewable size or crosses new scope boundaries, surface an advisory split recommendation rather than blocking apply.

guard_lines: Chained PRs: advisory only, not required before apply
Keep slice commits and rollback checkpoints visible

reviewable_slices.name: Provider compatibility and config preflight
reviewable_slices.goal: Prove the selected AI SDK provider path can be configured and mocked without changing /api/chat behavior.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Remove AI SDK dependency/config modules and keep existing azureResponsesClient path untouched.

reviewable_slices.name: Adapter extraction with legacy Azure runtime
reviewable_slices.goal: Move model calls behind provider-neutral back/services/agentRuntime while preserving behavior.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Switch chat.ts and guardrailNarration.ts imports back to azureResponsesClient; leave tests documenting behavior.

reviewable_slices.name: AI SDK text runtime behind flag
reviewable_slices.goal: Enable plain text streaming/generation through AI SDK behind AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=ai_sdk while preserving custom SSE.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Set AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=azure_responses and keep aiSdkRuntime isolated.

reviewable_slices.name: Custom SSE alert protocol cleanup
reviewable_slices.goal: Formalize event: alert parsing/display so backend safety notices are not silently ignored.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Revert frontend alert parser/callback changes and convert backend alert emissions to token text as fallback if needed.

reviewable_slices.name: Read-only AI SDK tool loop
reviewable_slices.goal: Allow holdings and USDC/SOL quote tools to execute server-side and synthesize grounded prose without proposal mutation.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Disable read-only AI SDK tool loop and fall back to existing backend tool routing.

reviewable_slices.name: Critical intent migration
reviewable_slices.goal: Use AI SDK-compatible schemas for transfer, swap, and conditional-buy intent detection only, then route through deterministic guardrail handlers.
reviewable_slices.rollback_boundary: Disable AI SDK critical intent detection and keep deterministic parser/current tool routing.

tasks.title: Add failing agent runtime config tests
actions: Create tests for AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER defaulting to legacy azure_responses.
Create tests for ai_sdk provider requiring a server-only model/provider configuration after provider choice is verified.
Create no-go tests proving missing model/config fails closed without logging secrets.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail because back/services/agentRuntime/config.ts does not exist yet.
Test names explicitly cover provider fallback, ai_sdk config, and secret redaction/no-go behavior.
tasks.rollback: Delete the new failing test file if the migration is cancelled before implementation.

tasks.title: Install and verify AI SDK provider dependency path
actions: Run npm install for ai and the selected provider package, installing only the minimal packages needed for the provider compatibility spike.
After install, inspect current AI SDK/provider docs and source in node_modules before coding against AI SDK APIs.
Verify whether existing Azure Responses/OpenAI-compatible envs can be used safely or whether Vercel AI Gateway is required.
Record the selected provider path, required envs, and no-go criteria in docs/ai-sdk-agent-migration/provider-decision.md.
Implement minimal back/services/agentRuntime/config.ts sufficient to pass T001 without exposing secrets.
acceptance_checks: package.json and package-lock.json contain only necessary AI SDK/provider dependencies.
Provider decision documents Azure-compatible AI SDK vs AI Gateway and rollback/no-go criteria.
Config tests pass and no provider secret appears in thrown messages or snapshots.
tasks.rollback: Remove added dependencies and config module; keep existing Azure Responses client unchanged.

tasks.title: Add mocked generation compatibility test
actions: Create a failing backend test using vi.mock for the selected AI SDK provider call to prove a tiny plain-text generation can be normalized into provider-neutral output.
Keep the test fully mocked; do not call external model services in CI.
acceptance_checks: Test fails before aiSdkRuntime.ts exists or before the mocked provider result is normalized.
Test verifies provider internals are not exported to chat.ts callers.
tasks.rollback: Delete the compatibility test if provider path is no-go.

tasks.title: Implement minimal mocked AI SDK runtime normalization
actions: Add provider-neutral AgentRuntime and AgentTextResult/AgentTextDelta types.
Implement a minimal aiSdkRuntime function that maps mocked provider text to normalized output only; do not wire chat.ts yet.
Keep all imports server-side under back/services/agentRuntime.
acceptance_checks: T003 passes with mocked provider data.
No frontend or /api/chat contract changes are present in this slice.
tasks.rollback: Delete aiSdkRuntime.ts/types/index.ts and retain azureResponsesClient.

tasks.title: Tighten provider preflight documentation and rollback marker
actions: Refine test descriptions and provider-decision notes so future reviewers can see why the provider path is safe or no-go.
Confirm that frontend protocol, guardrails, and secret boundaries remain unchanged.
acceptance_checks: Provider decision includes selected path, required env vars, rollback/no-go criteria, and date.
All S1 tests pass without external network/model calls.
tasks.rollback: Revert provider-decision.md edits if the preflight is abandoned.

tasks.title: Add failing tests for legacy adapter extraction
actions: Add tests proving azureResponsesClient outputs map to provider-neutral text, stream deltas, and tool intent records.
Update chat.test expectations so chat.ts can mock back/services/agentRuntime instead of importing azureResponsesClient internals.
Add a guardrailNarration test proving narration uses an adapter seam or intentionally isolated runtime wrapper.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail before azureResponsesRuntime and runtime factory are implemented.
Tests preserve existing transcript, token, proposal, done, and error behavior.
tasks.rollback: Remove new extraction tests and keep current azureResponsesClient mocks.

tasks.title: Create legacy Azure runtime adapter and runtime factory
actions: Wrap callAzureResponses, callAzureResponsesStream, and parseResponsesStream behavior behind AgentRuntime methods.
Default createAgentRuntime/getAgentRuntime to azure_responses unless AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER is set and valid.
Keep azureResponsesClient.ts as the rollback implementation; do not delete it.
acceptance_checks: Legacy adapter tests pass.
Runtime factory returns legacy adapter by default.
No critical action behavior changes are introduced.
tasks.rollback: Point runtime factory back to direct azureResponsesClient usage or delete agentRuntime folder.

tasks.title: Wire chat orchestration and guardrail narration through adapter seam
actions: Replace direct imports from back/services/azureResponsesClient.ts in chat.ts with runtime adapter calls.
Preserve parseDirectTransferIntent execution before any runtime/model call.
Route narration model calls through a dedicated adapter seam without mutating official guardrail explanation data.
Keep /api/chat JSON request types approval, result, reject, and history free from AI SDK runtime calls.
acceptance_checks: chat.test passes with runtime adapter mocks instead of azureResponsesClient mocks.
Direct transfer tests prove the runtime is not called when deterministic parser matches.
guardrailNarration tests still prove sanitized data only and provider failures return undefined.
tasks.rollback: Revert chat.ts and guardrailNarration.ts imports to azureResponsesClient; leave adapter files unused.

tasks.title: Add behavior-preservation regression tests around adapter seam
actions: Add tests for assistant text persistence from terminal stream done events through the adapter.
Add tests for provider error normalization to existing error SSE events.
Add tests proving one active pendingProposal is not altered by ordinary text streaming.
acceptance_checks: Regression tests pass and preserve existing custom SSE semantics.
No test requires AI SDK UI streams or frontend useChat.
tasks.rollback: Remove added regression tests if they duplicate stronger existing coverage.

tasks.title: Consolidate adapter types and remove direct Azure coupling from tests
actions: Tighten AgentRuntime types to avoid ambiguous any values where practical.
Keep azureResponsesClient tests focused on legacy config/client behavior only.
Ensure chat tests mock provider-neutral runtime output, not provider-specific Azure payloads.
acceptance_checks: No direct azureResponsesClient import remains in chat.ts or guardrailNarration.ts.
Legacy client remains covered and available for rollback.
tasks.rollback: Restore previous test mocks if adapter seam introduces review blockers.

tasks.title: Add failing tests for AI SDK text streaming behind feature flag
actions: Add mocked tests for AI SDK text delta normalization into AgentTextDelta values.
Add chat tests proving AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=ai_sdk maps deltas to event: token and emits exactly one event: done on success.
Add tests proving provider errors fail closed and map to existing error event semantics.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail before aiSdkRuntime streaming implementation is complete and wired through runtime factory.
Tests do not permit AI SDK UI stream chunks to escape to the frontend.
tasks.rollback: Delete or skip ai_sdk provider tests if provider decision is no-go.

tasks.title: Implement AI SDK plain text runtime behind AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER
actions: Implement mocked-test-compatible text streaming/generation using the verified AI SDK/provider API.
Wire runtime factory so ai_sdk is selected only when AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=ai_sdk and config is valid.
Translate AI SDK text deltas to existing Compass token SSE events in chat.ts; do not change /api/chat request or response types.
acceptance_checks: AI SDK text runtime tests pass with vi.mock provider stubs.
chat.ts still emits only custom SSE events for text turns.
Default runtime remains azure_responses unless feature flag selects ai_sdk.
tasks.rollback: Set AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=azure_responses or revert aiSdkRuntime/index wiring.

tasks.title: Add security and masking regressions for AI SDK runtime calls
actions: Add tests proving Solana addresses are masked before model input and restored only for deterministic handler/tool args.
Add tests proving provider config errors do not include API keys, auth headers, or raw provider payloads.
Add tests proving approval/reject/result/history requests do not invoke AI SDK runtime.
acceptance_checks: Security regressions pass under both azure_responses and ai_sdk provider selection.
No private key, signed transaction, or provider secret can enter prompt/test snapshots.
tasks.rollback: Retain tests and disable ai_sdk provider if failures reveal unsafe behavior.

tasks.title: Clean runtime factory boundaries without changing behavior
actions: Minimize provider-specific imports to aiSdkRuntime.ts and azureResponsesRuntime.ts.
Ensure chat.ts imports only provider-neutral types/factory functions.
Keep runtime creation testable and side-effect-light.
acceptance_checks: No AI SDK provider API is imported by chat.ts, app/api/chat/route.ts, or frontend files.
All S3 tests pass after refactor.
tasks.rollback: Revert only refactor commits; keep tested runtime behavior intact.

tasks.title: Add failing frontend/API tests for streamed alert events
actions: Add streamChat test for event: alert parsing and callback dispatch.
Add hook/store tests proving alert messages render as non-approvable chat messages and do not set pendingProposal.
Keep existing proposal approval/signing/guard-bypass tests unchanged.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail before client/hook alert stream handling is implemented.
Tests distinguish alert from proposal and token events.
tasks.rollback: Remove alert tests and choose backend conversion-to-token fallback if frontend change is not approved.

tasks.title: Implement typed alert SSE parsing and UI state handling
actions: Parse event: alert in streamChat using existing alert schemas/types where possible.
Propagate alert events through useAgentMessage into chatStore as non-approvable messages.
Ensure alert does not set awaiting approval, pending proposal, transaction payload, or risk acceptance state.
acceptance_checks: Frontend alert tests pass.
Existing proposal and wallet signing tests continue to pass.
No AI SDK useChat migration is introduced.
tasks.rollback: Revert frontend alert handling and use backend token fallback for safety notices.

tasks.title: Document /api/chat alert stream contract and backend coverage
actions: Update docs/api-reference.md to include event: alert as a typed non-approvable safety/info notice.
Add backend test for conditional funding or approval path that emits alert and still emits terminal done/error correctly.
Verify proposal remains the only approvable UI event.
acceptance_checks: API docs include alert payload shape and non-approvable semantics.
Backend alert test passes and frontend parser test covers the same shape.
tasks.rollback: Revert docs/test additions if alert support is deferred and convert backend alerts to supported token text.

tasks.title: Reduce duplicate alert schema/type handling
actions: Consolidate alert parsing/type conversion with existing history alert types to avoid drift.
Keep rendering through existing front/src/components/chat/AlertBanner.tsx and MessageList.tsx behavior.
acceptance_checks: No duplicate incompatible alert payload types remain.
All S4 tests pass after refactor.
tasks.rollback: Revert only schema/type refactor if it introduces churn.

tasks.title: Add failing tests for read-only AI SDK holdings and quote tools
actions: Add tests for get_wallet_holdings using session/authenticated wallet as source of truth, not model-supplied wallet override.
Add tests for get_usdc_sol_quote minimized result shape with network, source, fetchedAt, quoteOnly, input/output tokens, and amount.
Add chat tests proving read-only turns synthesize natural-language token text and do not create or mutate pendingProposal.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail before readOnlyTools.ts and runReadOnlyTurn are implemented.
Tests verify raw JSON is not emitted as the final assistant answer for normal user requests.
tasks.rollback: Delete read-only tool loop tests if tool-loop scope is postponed.

tasks.title: Implement executable read-only AI SDK tools and synthesis turn
actions: Define AI SDK executable tools only for get_wallet_holdings and get_usdc_sol_quote.
Call existing backend services for holdings and quotes; normalize/minimize results before model synthesis.
Implement runReadOnlyTurn with bounded steps/tool calls and no critical tools in the executable tools map.
Emit synthesized prose through existing token events.
acceptance_checks: Holdings and quote read-only tool tests pass.
No proposal event or pendingProposal mutation occurs from read-only turns.
Quote responses identify quote-only nature and source/freshness when available.
tasks.rollback: Disable runReadOnlyTurn routing and keep existing read-only behavior.

tasks.title: Add failure/no-hallucination and data-minimization regressions
actions: Add tests for holdings provider failure, quote provider failure, timeout, and inconsistent network/mint data.
Verify assistant states fresh data is unavailable or incomplete and does not invent balances, prices, routes, or executable amounts.
Verify only request-relevant portfolio fields are passed to model synthesis.
acceptance_checks: Failure tests pass with grounded non-hallucinated responses.
Tool result payloads exclude unnecessary full holdings dumps and sensitive wallet data.
tasks.rollback: Keep tests as safety gates and disable tool loop if implementation cannot satisfy them.

tasks.title: Consolidate read-only schemas and prompts
actions: Deduplicate Zod/schema definitions for read-only tool inputs/results.
Keep prompt/synthesis instructions focused on quote-only, freshness, and no-hallucination constraints.
Ensure read-only tools remain testable without external provider/model calls.
acceptance_checks: Read-only tool schemas are shared and typed without leaking AI SDK details to chat.ts.
All S5 tests pass after refactor.
tasks.rollback: Revert prompt/schema refactor while preserving working tool loop.

tasks.title: Add failing tests for critical intent schemas and deterministic routing
actions: Add tests for transfer, swap_orca_usdc_to_sol, and conditional_buy_sol intent schema validation with no side-effecting execute function.
Add chat tests proving model-detected critical intents route into existing deterministic handlers and emit proposal only after guardrails pass.
Add tests for malformed, incomplete, ambiguous, multiple, or unsafe critical intents failing closed with clarification/error and no transaction preparation.
acceptance_checks: Tests fail before criticalIntents.ts/toolDefinitions intent-only support is implemented.
Tests explicitly prove critical tools are not included in executable read-only AI SDK tools map.
tasks.rollback: Remove critical intent tests if this slice is postponed and keep deterministic/current routing only.

tasks.title: Implement critical intent-only definitions and chat routing
actions: Define AI SDK-compatible intent schemas for transfer, swap_orca_usdc_to_sol, and conditional_buy_sol without side-effecting execute functions.
Normalize detected AgentToolIntent records and route them through existing handleTransferToolCall, handleOrcaSwapToolCall, and handleConditionalBuyToolCall semantics after address restoration.
Enforce at most one active pendingProposal and reject multiple critical intents in one turn unless the user chooses one action.
acceptance_checks: Critical intent tests pass.
Critical intent cannot create an approvable proposal until deterministic backend validation succeeds.
No unsigned transaction is returned until explicit function_approve.
tasks.rollback: Disable AI SDK critical intent routing and leave direct parser/current deterministic handlers active.

tasks.title: Add guardrail preservation regressions for transfer, swap, and conditional buy
actions: Verify deterministic direct-transfer parser still short-circuits before runtime intent detection.
Verify transfer approval still validates wallet ownership, action hash, policy PDA, attestation, expiry, and guard readiness.
Verify swap guard_rejected_awaiting_bypass still requires explicit second function_approve with accept_risk.
Verify conditional funding result can create the conditional-order proposal only after the expected signed result is confirmed.
acceptance_checks: All critical flow preservation tests pass under the ai_sdk runtime flag and legacy runtime where applicable.
Model-generated text cannot count as risk acceptance or approval.
tasks.rollback: Keep regressions and disable critical AI SDK intent if any guardrail invariant fails.

tasks.title: Finalize critical intent boundaries and remove ambiguous coupling
actions: Keep critical intent normalization separate from transaction preparation and proposal state mutation.
Ensure chat.ts remains the only owner of proposal lifecycle and SSE translation.
Remove any duplicate critical schema declarations that could drift from deterministic handler validation.
acceptance_checks: No critical tool has an AI SDK executable function that can perform side effects.
All S6 tests pass after refactor.
tasks.rollback: Revert refactor only; keep passing critical routing behavior or disable ai_sdk critical intent.

tasks.title: Run full non-deferred verification and acceptance checklist
actions: Run backend, frontend, lint, build, and typecheck commands after all non-deferred slices.
Confirm app/api/chat/route.ts remains thin and no browser-side file imports back/services directly.
Confirm custom SSE protocol remains session/token/proposal/alert/done/error and no useChat migration is present.
Confirm docs/api-reference.md reflects any finalized /api/chat SSE contract changes.
acceptance_checks: npm run test:back passes.
npm test passes.
npm run lint passes.
npm run build passes.
npx tsc --noEmit passes.
Acceptance criteria from functional-spec.md are covered or explicitly deferred for sandbox/useChat.
tasks.rollback: If verification fails on provider-specific behavior, set AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER=azure_responses and revert the failing slice rather than weakening guardrails.

deferred_optional_tasks.title: Per-user persistent sandbox service
deferred_optional_tasks.reason_deferred: Functional and technical specs say sandboxes should be added only after a concrete product workflow requires filesystem or command execution.
tdd_outline: RED: tests for same authenticated owner resolving same salted/hashed sandbox name, different owners isolated, missing owner refused, raw wallet/PII absent, policy denies signing/secrets/arbitrary commands.
GREEN: add @vercel/sandbox and userSandbox service behind explicit workflow flag with no production secrets or custody authority.
TRIANGULATE: add timeout/output/network-deny and snapshot-expiration tests using mocks.

deferred_optional_tasks.title: Frontend AI SDK useChat evaluation or migration
deferred_optional_tasks.reason_deferred: Initial migration must keep custom SSE and current approval/signature UX. useChat should be evaluated only after backend semantics are stable.
tdd_outline: RED: tests proving proposal cards, wallet signing, explicit risk acceptance, and result callbacks remain clear under any candidate UI protocol.
GREEN: migrate only if typed AI SDK UI messages reduce coupling without hiding guardrail state.
TRIANGULATE: wallet mismatch, stale proposal, and guard-bypass regressions.
REFACTOR: simplify chat store only after behavior parity is proven.

global_acceptance_checks: /api/chat request and custom SSE contract remain compatible during initial migration.
app/api/chat/route.ts remains a thin route handler.
back/services/chat.ts remains owner of session/proposal lifecycle, approval, rejection, result, history, and SSE translation.
parseDirectTransferIntent runs before runtime/model calls for supported direct transfer phrases.
Critical actions are AI SDK intent-only and always route through deterministic Compass guardrails.
Frontend signing remains self-custodial; backend never receives private keys or signed transactions for execution.
Read-only holdings and quote tools use backend-managed data, minimize model payloads, include freshness/source context, and do not mutate pendingProposal.
Alert stream events are either formally handled as typed non-approvable events or converted to supported event types before protocol stability is claimed.
Provider selection is reversible at AGENT_RUNTIME_PROVIDER and defaults to legacy azure_responses until ai_sdk is proven.
No sandbox or useChat implementation is included without separate approval.

risks.risk: AI SDK provider incompatibility with existing Azure Responses path
risks.mitigation: Run S1 first, keep legacy adapter default, record provider decision/no-go criteria, and avoid frontend/protocol changes.

risks.risk: Autonomous tool loop could bypass guardrails
risks.mitigation: Executable AI SDK tools are limited to read-only holdings/quote; critical schemas are intent-only and deterministic handlers own side effects.

risks.risk: Single PR becomes hard to review because it spans dependencies, backend adapter, frontend SSE, and docs
risks.mitigation: Preserve the single-PR default with clear slice commits and rollback checkpoints; surface splitting only as an advisory if review clarity degrades.

risks.risk: Frontend alert protocol cleanup adds cross-boundary scope
risks.mitigation: Keep it as its own slice with frontend tests first; no AI SDK useChat migration.

risks.risk: Model receives excessive wallet portfolio data
risks.mitigation: Add data-minimization tests and restrict read-only tool result shapes to request-relevant fields.

engram_record.reason: No Engram tool is available in the current tool namespace. Significant decisions/discoveries are captured in this task.json for the parent/orchestrator to persist to Engram if needed.
decisions_to_record: Strict TDD is active; tests must precede implementation for backend/API/frontend runtime changes.
Single PR remains the default; if review clarity degrades because the work spans dependency, backend adapter, frontend SSE, and docs boundaries, recommend splitting as advisory only.
Sandbox and frontend useChat are deferred unless separately approved or required by a concrete workflow.
Provider migration must be reversible with azure_responses as default until AI SDK compatibility is proven.
